description.task: Implement the same database operation (save and retrieve a user) using two approaches: raw JDBC simulation and ORM simulation. Compare the code complexity of each.
instructions: Add getters getId(), getName(), getEmail() to User.
JdbcUserDao.save() stores {name, email} array in table map and prints the SQL.
JdbcUserDao.findById() prints 'JDBC: SELECT * FROM users WHERE id=N', retrieves from table, returns new User(id, name, email) or null.
Run Main as-is — the rest is already provided.
description.hint: Spring JDBC Template reduces JDBC boilerplate by 80%. Spring Data JPA (ORM) eliminates even more with auto-generated queries.

description.task: Build a Spring Data-inspired repository hierarchy. Create base interfaces and their implementations to understand how CrudRepository, JpaRepository, and PagingAndSortingRepository relate.
instructions: Implement all methods of CustomerRepository: save, findById, findAll, delete (by object), count, flush (print 'Flushing to database'), saveAll, pagination, and sorting.
findAll(page, pageSize) uses page as 0-based page index.
Expected output shows count, paged result, and sorted names.
description.hint: Spring Data JPA's JpaRepository extends CrudRepository and PagingAndSortingRepository. You get all these methods for free — Spring generates the implementation.
description.expectedOutput:
Count: 4
Page 1 (size 2): [Customer{1, Alice}, Customer{2, Bob}]
Sorted: [Customer{1, Alice}, Customer{2, Bob}, Customer{3, Charlie}, Customer{4, Diana}]

description.task: Implement a simple database connection pool in Java. The pool pre-creates connections and lends them out to callers, returning them after use.
description.context: Spring's DataSource manages a connection pool (HikariCP by default). Connection pooling is critical for performance — creating DB connections is expensive.
instructions: In constructor, create maxSize MockConnections, add each to both available queue and allConnections list.
borrow() polls from available, marks inUse=true, returns it. If available is empty, throw RuntimeException.
returnConnection() marks inUse=false and offers back to available.
description.hint: HikariCP is Spring Boot's default connection pool. It maintains a pool of pre-created connections to eliminate the overhead of creating a new DB connection per request.
description.expectedOutput:
Pool: 0/3 connections in use
Pool: 2/3 connections in use
[Conn#1] Result of: SELECT * FROM orders
Pool: 1/3 connections in use
